texto: Later that afternoon, the girls sat on the porch, bored and worried. It was good that they didn't find any dead bats, but why didn't they? Was Tyler right - did the bats fly higher when it was windier? Was it because they could avoid the turbine easier when it was spinning faster? That didn't make sense.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Pamela McDowell que provoca esforço, persistência e resultados. Ao questionar “Later that afternoon, the girls sat on the porch, bored and worried. It was good that they didn't…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre familia, children-s-book, nature, com foco em nature.
